info.title: Zenkit API
info.description: Zenkit is a platform for collaboration and project management. This API provides programmatic access to workspaces, lists, entries, elements, activities, notifications, and more within the Zenkit platform.

post.summary: Register a new OAuth client.
post.description: Register a new OAuth client.

get.summary:
Endpoint to redirect users to during the Authorization Code Grant flow.
The user will be prompted to allow your app acce
get.description:
Endpoint to redirect users to during the Authorization Code Grant flow.
The user will be prompted to allow your app access to their data.
If successfull, the Authentication Code will be sent back to your client at the Redirect URI.
If no redirect URI is provided here, the one set up during client registration will be used.
If a redirect URI is provided here, it has to match the one provided during client registration.
However, it may include additional query parameters.
If your app provides a state parameter its contents will be sent back in subsequent calls of the Authorization Code Grant flow.

post.summary:
Endpoint to exchange an Authorization Code for an Access Token.
The client_id and client_secret may be passed as body pa
post.description:
Endpoint to exchange an Authorization Code for an Access Token.
The client_id and client_secret may be passed as body parameters or as part of HTTP Basic Authentication.
This endpoint may also be used to receive subsequent Access Tokens via your Refresh Token without having to ask the user for authorization.

post.summary:
Get the most recently updated list entries of a list using a watermark. The first call should not include a watermark.
F
post.description:
Get the most recently updated list entries of a list using a watermark. The first call should not include a watermark.
From there on, send along the watermark that the server returns.
Every call may include the timestamp of the first synchronization call via excludeDeprecatedBefore,
in order to ignore entries that were deprecated before the synchronization even started.

schema.description: Can be ID, ShortID, or UUID
